title: Shorea helferi
intro: Shorea helferi, also known as Helfer's dipterocarp, is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae. The plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, potentially providing relief from fever, cough, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Shorea helferi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Shorea helferi is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the production of traditional herbal remedies such as ointments and poultices.
taxonomy: Shorea helferi belongs to the family Dipterocarpaceae. This plant is a species within the genus Shorea. Shorea helferi is native to Southeast Asia. It is a type of tree that is often found in tropical rainforests. This species is also known as Shorea helferi is a part of the order Malvales.
uses: Shorea helferi is a species of plant that has been found to have various medicinal properties. Its bark and leaves have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and digestive issues. It is also used to treat skin conditions and wounds due to its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties. Furthermore, it has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and swelling. Research on Shorea helferi is ongoing to fully explore its potential medicinal uses and benefits.
